title: Multi-link low latency relaying
abstract: A first station (STA) receives a first portion of a first physical layer protocol data unit (PPDU) via a first link from a second STA. Based on the first portion of the first PPDU indicating low latency relaying and comprising an address of the first STA as a first receiver address of the first PPDU, the first STA decodes and forwards a second portion of the first PPDU for transmission in a second PPDU via a second link to a third STA.
claims:
Claims Docket No.: 23-3020PCT [0270] In an embodiment, the first portion may comprise a portion of a first data unit of the first PPDU. In an embodiment, the first data unit may comprise a first MPDU of the first PPDU. In an embodiment, process 3200 may further comprise performing a frame check sequence (FCS) of a first data unit of the first PPDU. [0271] In an embodiment, the first portion may comprise a portion of a PHY header of the first PPDU and/or a portion of a MAC header comprised in the first PPDU. The MAC header may be a MAC header of a data unit of the first PPDU. The data unit may comprise a low latency data frame. In an embodiment, the first portion may be in the MAC header comprised in the first PPDU. [0272] In an embodiment, the second portion may comprise a second data unit of the first PPDU. In an embodiment, process 3200 may further comprise setting the destination address of the first PPDU as a receiver address in a MAC header of at least one data unit of the second PPDU. In another embodiment, setting the destination address of the first PPDU as a receiver address in a MAC header of at least one data unit of the second PPDU may comprise setting the destination address of the first PPDU as a receiver address in MAC headers of all data units of the second PPDU. In an embodiment, process 3200 may further comprise forwarding the first portion of the first PPDU for transmission in the second PPDU via the second link to the third STA, while receiving the second portion of the first PPDU via the first link from the second STA. [0273] In an embodiment, process 3200 may further comprise transmitting, by the first STA to the second STA, an ACK frame via the first link, after a successful check of FCSs of all data units of the first PPDU. In another embodiment, process 3200 may further comprise not transmitting, by the first STA to the second STA, an ACK frame via the first link, after a failed check of an FCS of at least one data unit of the first PPDU. Docket No.: 23-3020PCT CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: receiving, by a first station (STA), a physical layer (PHY) header of a first physical layer protocol data unit (PPDU) via a first link from a second STA; and based on the PHY header of the first PPDU indicating low latency relaying and comprising an address of the first STA as a first receiver address of the first PPDU, decoding and forwarding a first portion of the first PPDU for transmission in a second PPDU via a second link to a third STA. 2. A method, comprising: receiving, by a first station (STA), a first portion of a first physical layer protocol data unit (PPDU) via a first link from a second STA; and based on the first portion of the first PPDU indicating low latency relaying and comprising an address of the first STA as a first receiver address of the first PPDU, decoding and forwarding a second portion of the first PPDU for transmission in a second PPDU via a second link to a third STA. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the first portion comprises a portion of a physical layer (PHY) header of the first PPDU. 4. The method of any of claims 2-3, wherein the first portion comprises an indication of low latency relaying. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the indication comprises a flag. 6. The method of any of claims 2-5, wherein the first portion comprises a destination address of the first PPDU or a destination address of a data unit of the first PPDU. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the first portion indicates low latency relaying based on the destination address being different than the first receiver address. 8. The method of any of claims 3-7, wherein the second portion comprises a portion of the PHY header of the first PPDU or a medium access control (MAC) header of the data unit of the first PPDU. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the second portion comprises the MAC header. 

Docket No.: 23-3020PCT 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the second portion comprises a second receiver address. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the second receiver address is equal to a destination address of the first PPDU or to the destination address of the data unit comprised in the first PPDU. 12. The method of any of claims 9-11, wherein forwarding the second portion of the first PPDU for transmission in the second PPDU does not comprise modifying the second portion. 13. The method of any of claims 2-12, further comprising decoding and forwarding the second portion of the first PPDU for transmission in the second PPDU via the second link to the third STA, while receiving the first PPDU via the first link from the second STA. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising transmitting a physical layer (PHY) header of the second PPDU before transmitting the second portion of the first PPDU in the second PPDU. 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising decoding and forwarding a third portion of the first PPDU for transmission in the second PPDU via the second link to the third STA, after transmitting the second portion of the first PPDU in the second PPDU. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the third portion of the first PPDU is a portion of a frame body of a first medium access control (MAC) protocol data unit (MPDU) of the first PPDU. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the first PPDU comprises an MPDU. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the MPDU is longer than 1ms. 19. The method of claim 15, further comprising transmitting, by the first STA to the second STA, an acknowledgment (ACK) frame via the first link, after a successful check of a frame check sequence (FCS) of the first PPDU. 20. The method of claim 15, further comprising not transmitting, by the first STA to the second STA, an ACK frame via the first link, after a failed check of a frame check sequence (FCS) of the first PPDU. 21. A method, comprising: 

Docket No.: 23-3020PCT transmitting, by a first station (STA), a first physical layer protocol data unit (PPDU) via a first link to a second STA, wherein the first PPDU comprises a first portion indicating low latency relaying and comprising an address of the second STA as a first receiver address of the first PPDU. 22. The method of any of claims 21-25, wherein the first PPDU further comprises a second portion. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the second portion comprises a portion of the physical layer (PHY) header of the first PPDU or a medium access control (MAC) header of the data unit of the first PPDU. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the second portion comprises the MAC header. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the second portion comprises a second receiver address. 30. The method of any of claims 21-30, wherein the first PPDU further comprises a frame body of a first medium access control (MAC) protocol data unit (MPDU). 32. A method, comprising: receiving, by a first station (STA), a first portion of a first physical layer protocol data unit (PPDU) via a first link from a second STA, wherein the first portion comprises a portion of a physical layer (PHY) header of the first PPDU and a portion of a medium access control (MAC) header comprised in the first PPDU; and 

Docket No.: 23-3020PCT based on the first portion indicating low latency relaying and comprising an address of the first STA as a receiver address of the first PPDU: decoding a second portion of the first PPDU to obtain a destination address of the first PPDU; and forwarding a third portion of the first PPDU for transmission in a second PPDU via a second link to a third STA corresponding to the destination address. 33. A method, comprising: receiving, by a first station (STA), a first portion of a first physical layer protocol data unit (PPDU) via a first link from a second STA; and based on the first portion of the first PPDU indicating low latency relaying and comprising an address of the first STA as a receiver address of the first PPDU: decoding a second portion of the first PPDU to obtain a destination address of the first PPDU; and forwarding a third portion of the first PPDU for transmission in a second PPDU via a second link to a third STA corresponding to the destination address. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the first portion comprises a portion of a physical layer (PHY) header of the first PPDU and a portion of a medium access control (MAC) header comprised in the first PPDU. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein the first portion comprises an indication of low latency relaying in the PHY header of the first PPDU or in the MAC header comprised in the first PPDU. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein the indication comprises a flag. 37. The method of any of claims 34-36, wherein the receiver address of the first PPDU is in the MAC header comprised in the first PPDU. 38. The method of claim 34, wherein the second portion comprises a portion of the MAC header comprised in the first PPDU. 39. The method of claim 38, wherein the second portion comprises the destination address of the first PPDU or of the data unit. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the destination address of the first PPDU is not equal to the receiver address of the first PPDU of the data unit. 

Docket No.: 23-3020PCT 41. The method of any of claims 33-40, further comprising setting the destination address of the first PPDU or of the data unit as a receiver address of the second PPDU. 42. The method of any of claims 33-41, wherein the third portion of the first PPDU comprises a portion of a frame body of a first medium access control (MAC) protocol data unit (MPDU) of the first PPDU. 43. A method, comprising: transmitting, by a first station (STA), a first physical layer protocol data unit (PPDU) via a first link to a second STA, wherein the first PPDU comprises a first portion indicating low latency relaying and comprising an address of the second STA as a receiver address of the first PPDU. 50. The method of claim 49, wherein the first portion comprises a portion of a physical layer (PHY) header of the first PPDU and a portion of a medium access control (MAC) header of the data unit of the first PPDU of the first PPDU. 51. The method of claim 50, wherein the first portion comprises an indication of low latency relaying in the PHY header or in the MAC header. 

Docket No.: 23-3020PCT 52. The method of claim 51, wherein the indication comprises a flag. 53. The method of any of claims 49-53, wherein the first PPDU further comprises a second portion. 55. The method of claim 55, wherein the second portion comprises a destination address of the first PPDU or the destination address of the data unit of the first PPDU. 57. The method of claim 56, wherein the destination address of the first PPDU is not equal to the receiver address of the first PPDU. 58. The method of any of claims 49-57, wherein the first PPDU further comprises a third portion. 59. The method of claim 58, wherein the third portion of the first PPDU comprises a portion of a frame body of a first medium access control (MAC) protocol data unit (MPDU) of the first PPDU. 60. A method, comprising: receiving, by a first station (STA), a first portion of a first physical layer protocol data unit (PPDU) via a first link from a second STA; and based on the first portion of the first PPDU indicating: low latency relaying, an address of the first STA as a receiver address for all data units of the first PPDU, and an address of a third STA as a destination address for all data units of the first PPDU, decoding and forwarding a second portion of the first PPDU for transmission in a second PPDU via a second link to a third STA. 61. The method of claim 60, wherein the first portion comprises a portion of a first data unit of the first PPDU. 62. The method of claim 60, wherein the first portion comprises an indication: 

Docket No.: 23-3020PCT of low latency relaying, that the receiver address is a same receiver address for all data units of the first PPDU, and that the destination address is a same destination address for all data units of the first PPDU, and wherein the first portion comprises a portion of a physical layer (PHY) header of the first PPDU or a portion of a medium access control (MAC) header comprised in the first PPDU. 63. The method of claim 62, wherein the indication of low latency relaying, that the receiver address is the same for all data units of the first PPDU, and that the destination address is the same for all data units of the first PPDU comprises a flag. 64. The method of claim 61, wherein the first portion is in a medium access control (MAC) header comprised in the first PPDU. 65. The method of any of claims 60-65, wherein the second portion comprises a second data unit of the first PPDU. 67. The method of any of claims 60-66, further comprising setting the destination address of the first PPDU as a receiver address in a medium access control (MAC) header of at least one data unit of the second PPDU. 68. The method of claim 67, wherein setting the destination address of the first PPDU as a receiver address in a MAC header of at least one data unit of the second PPDU comprises setting the destination address of the first PPDU as a receiver address in MAC headers of all data units of the second PPDU. 69. The method of any of claims 60-68, further comprising forwarding the first portion of the first PPDU for transmission in the second PPDU via the second link to the third STA, while receiving the second portion of the first PPDU via the first link from the second STA. 70. The method of claim 69, further comprising transmitting, by the first STA to the second STA, an ACK frame via the first link, after a successful check of frame check sequences (FCSs) of all data units of the first PPDU. 71. The method of claim 69, further comprising not transmitting, by the first STA to the second STA, an ACK frame via the first link, after a failed check of a frame check sequence (FCS) of any of data units of the first PPDU.
